title: Why Mānuka isn't the only healing honey from New Zealand anymore
summary: New research from Auckland University of Technology reveals that eight native New Zealand honeys, beyond mānuka, possess unique antioxidant, antibacterial, and bioactive properties. Thyme and beech honeydew stood out for their vitamin C and polyphenol content.

title: Who was Katie Donnell? Heart attack kills Florida ‘workout queen' at 28, and her mom blames energy drinks
summary: Katie Donnell, 28, was committed to a healthy lifestyle but often drank three energy drinks a day, and “was drinking a lot of coffee,” according to her mother.

title: Stanford study reveals glucose's surprising role in cell differentiation
summary: The sugar glucose, which is the main source of energy in almost every living cell, has been revealed in a Stanford Medicine study to also be a master regulator of tissue differentiation - the process by which stem cells give rise to specialized cells that make up all the body's tissues.

title: Researchers unlock key process for regenerating intestinal cells after injury
summary: Researchers from the University of Colorado Cancer Center have solved a cellular mystery that may lead to better therapies for colorectal and other types of cancer.

title: Yale study unlocks potential of lupus-related antibody to target cold tumors
summary: Yale scientists have discovered a promising way to trigger immune responses against certain tumors, using a lupus-related antibody that can slip, undetected, into "cold" tumors and flip on an immune response that has been turned off by cancer.

title: Ministry of Health proposes allowing tissue donation for individuals aged 16 and older
summary: Individuals aged 16 and older may donate their tissues while alive, but only with written consent from their legal representative or guardian.

title: Postpartum females show shift in temperature preferences due to brain changes
summary: Mothers experience major metabolic adaptations during pregnancy and lactation to support the development and growth of the new life.

title: NUS researchers develop microneedle technology to accelerate diabetic wound healing
summary: Diabetic wounds often lead to severe complications that can result in amputations. These chronic and non-healing wounds are marked by persistent inflammation, affecting more than six per cent of the global population. In Singapore, there are about four lower limb amputations daily due to non-healing diabetic wounds. A study focusing on diabetic ...

title: The Weight Loss Paradox: Shedding Significant Pounds Can Be Deadly For Some
summary: One of the lasting memories from my teenage years is what I now recognise as an obsession with weight control.

title: Express view on Technology vs TB
summary: Use of cutting-edge technology can help overcome traditional weakness of tuberculosis care
